what_it_does: Defines an evidence-tagged set of design-element relationships, their observed spacing behavior, axes, provenance, confidence, boundaries, and next encoding work.
observations: Opens with an explicit purpose statement and provenance taxonomy for TIM, DECK, MOCKUP, CONTENT, and AGENT evidence.
Centers a resolution model where a stable relationship can resolve to different gaps by context.
Introduces direction and density modulation as observed relationship properties.
Groups relationships across within-element couplets, containment, temporal, type, and context axes.
Separates Tim-authored system laws from deck/mockup/content-observed element pairings.
Cross-checks an existing grammar relation set without adopting it as truth.
Ends with explicit limitations and pointers to related model, harvest, content, and journal materials.
standouts: Relationship is treated as constant while gap is treated as contextual output.
The file records provenance and confidence alongside each relationship claim.
It distinguishes element pairings from broader system laws.
It frames future work as roles, dials, and signed edges derived from these relationships.
conventions: Uses markdown sections, bullets, and one table to structure claims.
Uses uppercase provenance tags and confidence language throughout.
Uses arrows to express directed pairings such as figure → label and eyebrow/superhead → title.
Mixes measured pixel values, semantic meanings, provenance notes, and interpretation in compact entries.
Maintains an explicit boundary section for unratified or limited claims.
concerns: Several properties are marked MOCKUP-only or source-unconfirmed within the file.
Semantic-relationship generality is stated as resting on one fully-authored content tree.
The document mixes evidence, synthesis, canon comparison, and next-work notes in one file.
notes: Brief this as an evidence ledger for design relationships, contextual spacing resolution, and future edge/role encoding.
questions: What threshold determines when a relationship becomes encoded as a role or edge?
How should confidence be represented when multiple provenance tags disagree or remain unratified?
What exact schema will hold direction, density, surface, and tier for resolved gaps?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it presents harvested relationships with provenance, confidence, interpretation, limits, and next encoding pointers.
intention_signals: It seems intended to turn observed design material into a neutral relationship inventory that can later be encoded as roles, dials, and signed edges.
novelty: It treats spacing as the resolved output of relationship, direction, density, and surface rather than as a fixed value attached to elements.
contribution: Contributes a provenance-aware relationship vocabulary for design substrates, including directed couplets, containment, temporal order, type assignment, context selection, and measured spacing behavior that can be retained as interpretive ledger data.
summary_for_embedding: An evidence-tagged markdown ledger defining observed design relationships, contextual gap resolution, relationship axes, provenance, confidence, system-law separation, and file-local limitations.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for replacing ad hoc bond labels with grounded relationship claims that can feed later role, dial, and edge encoding.
